prediction_reason.历年位次与招生计划: 2021–2025年物理类最低位次分别为约16670名→17057名→16578名→17567名→17831名，整体呈波动后移趋势。2021至2022年小幅后移约387名，2023年略有回升至16 578名，但2024年起连续两年后移，2025年基准位次约17831名，近两年累计后移约1253名，表明报考竞争持续加剧；该校在福建省物理类招生计划2021–2024年 稳定在81–82人，2025年大幅增至109人（扩招约34.6%），2026年进一步扩至139人，较2025年再增30人（增幅约27.5%）。连续两年大幅扩招，显著增加了福建考生的 录取机会，对位次有下拉作用。
prediction_reason.学校层次与声誉: 太原理工大学为211工程、双一流建设高校，软科中国大学排名从2021年第113名持续上升至2026年第97名，排名稳步提升，反映 出学校综合实力和声誉的增强，对考生吸引力有所提升；2022年高考周期内曾出现偏负面声誉信号（涉及学校管理争议），但2026年声誉文件中无该校相关记录 ，表明近期无重大负面舆情。抖音综合热度排名第63位，评价为“一般”，短视频平台曝光量中等，未出现明显热度异常信号。历史投档异常记录中亦无该校断档记录，报考心理相 对平稳；第五轮学科评估中，化学工程与技术获评A-，为该校唯一A-及以上学科，与招生专业中的化学工程与工艺、精细化工等直接对应，对相关专业报考有一 定支撑。此外，机械、材料、电气等工科专业虽未获A类评级，但属该校传统优势方向。
prediction_reason.专业热度与地域: 计算机类、电气工程及其自动化、电子信息/通信等专业在2024–2 026年专业热度分析中均获“高热推荐”或“推荐”评级，社会关注度高，而该校在上述领域均有招生，有助于吸引高分考生。福建省物理类考生总数从2021年约11.7万持续增长至202 5年约19.2万，2026年估算约20.4万人（较2025年增长约6.1%），考生规模扩大加剧整体竞争。；太原理工大学位于山西省 太原市，距福建省较远，属于跨省报考。该校在福建的招生计划近年。山西作为中西部省份， 对东南沿海考生的地域吸引力相对有限，但扩招幅度较大可部分抵消地域偏好影响。
prediction_reason.预测结论: 综合以上因素：2026年招生计划较2025年再扩招27.5%，是影响位次的最主 要因素，大幅扩招将显著增加录取机会，使最低位次数字趋于增大（竞争缓解）；但福建省物理类考生增长约6.1%、学校软科排名持续上升至第97名、工科热门专业（计算机、电 气等）社会热度高，均对位次有上推作用。经综合权衡，预测2026年物理类最低位次约20576名，区间20076–21076名。

major_tier_recommendation.overall_advice: 预测位次约20576名，区间20076–21076名。建议位次在20000名以内的考生可重点冲刺一档专业（电气、计算机、机器人试验班等）；位次在20000–21000名之间的考生以二档专 业为主力填报区间，兼顾一档冲刺；位次在21000名以后的考生可重点考虑三档专业保底，同时关注二档中位次相对靠后的专业（如化工、材料等）。该校2026年大幅扩招，整体 录取机会较往年明显增加，建议考生结合自身位次合理布局冲稳保梯度。

历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for Physics Category were approximately 16670 → 17057 → 16578 → 17567 → 17831, showing an overall fluctuating and backward trend. From 2021 to 2022, there was a slight backward shift of about 387 places; in 2023, it slightly recovered to 16578, but from 2024 onward, it shifted backward for two consecutive years. In 2025, the baseline ranking was about 17831, with a cumulative backward shift of about 1253 places over the past two years, indicating intensifying competition for admission. The university's enrollment plan for Physics Category in Fujian Province was stable at 81–82 students from 2021 to 2024, but increased significantly to 109 students in 2025 (an expansion of about 34.6%), and further expanded to 139 students in 2026, an increase of 30 students compared to 2025 (an increase of about 27.5%). Two consecutive years of substantial expansion have significantly increased admission opportunities for Fujian candidates, exerting a downward pull on rankings.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: Taiyuan University of Technology is a 211 Project and Double First-Class construction university. Its ranking in the Shanghai Ranking's Chinese University Rankings has steadily risen from 113th in 2021 to 97th in 2026, reflecting an enhancement in the university's comprehensive strength and reputation, which increases its attractiveness to candidates. During the 2022 college entrance examination cycle, there were some negative reputation signals (involving university management controversies), but the 2026 reputation file contains no records related to the university, indicating no major negative public opinion recently. On Douyin, its comprehensive popularity ranking is 63rd, rated as 'average', with moderate exposure on short video platforms and no obvious abnormal popularity signals. There are also no records of historical admission anomalies such as gaps, indicating relatively stable application psychology. In the fifth round of discipline evaluation, Chemical Engineering and Technology was rated A-, the only A- or above discipline at the university, directly corresponding to majors such as Chemical Engineering and Technology and Fine Chemicals in the enrollment plan, providing some support for related major applications. Additionally, engineering majors such as Mechanical Engineering, Materials Science, and Electrical Engineering, though not rated as A-level, are traditional strengths of the university.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Geography
专业热度与地域.text: Majors such as Computer Science, Electrical Engineering and Automation, and Electronic Information/Communication have been rated as 'highly recommended' or 'recommended' in professional popularity analysis from 2024 to 2026, with high social attention. The university offers enrollment in these fields, helping to attract high-scoring candidates. The total number of Physics Category candidates in Fujian Province has grown from about 117,000 in 2021 to about 192,000 in 2025, with an estimated 204,000 in 2026 (an increase of about 6.1% compared to 2025). The expansion of the candidate pool intensifies overall competition. Taiyuan University of Technology is located in Taiyuan, Shanxi Province, which is far from Fujian Province, making it a cross-provincial application. The university's enrollment plan in Fujian has been expanding in recent years. As a central and western province, Shanxi has relatively limited geographical appeal to candidates from the southeastern coast, but the large expansion can partially offset the impact of geographical preferences.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the above factors: The 2026 enrollment plan expands by 27.5% compared to 2025, which is the most important factor affecting rankings. The substantial expansion will significantly increase admission opportunities, causing the minimum ranking number to increase (easing competition). However, the 6.1% increase in Physics Category candidates in Fujian Province, the university's continuous rise in Shanghai Ranking to 97th place, and the high social popularity of popular engineering majors (Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, etc.) all exert an upward push on rankings. After comprehensive weighing, the predicted minimum ranking for Physics Category in 2026 is approximately 20576, with a range of 20076–21076.

major_tier_recommendation_en.overall_advice: The predicted ranking is about 20576, with a range of 20076–21076. Candidates with rankings within 20000 are advised to focus on Tier 1 majors (Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, Robotics Experimental Class, etc.); candidates with rankings between 20000 and 21000 should mainly consider Tier 2 majors as the primary application range, while also attempting Tier 1 majors; candidates with rankings above 21000 can focus on Tier 3 majors as safety options, while also paying attention to Tier 2 majors with relatively lower rankings (such as Chemical Engineering, Materials Science, etc.). The university's significant expansion in 2026 means overall admission opportunities are notably higher than in previous years. Candidates are advised to reasonably arrange their application strategy of 'reach, match, and safety' based on their own rankings.
